title: Designing a Sentiment Analysis Model with Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis
scenario: Design a sentiment analysis model that uses aspect-based sentiment analysis to analyze product reviews and identify both the sentiment and the aspect of the product being discussed. The system must be able to handle large product review datasets, identify both positive and negative sentiments, and provide accurate aspect identification. The output should be a Python script (as code) implementing the aspect-based sentiment analysis algorithm. Constraints: Must use a NLP library (e.g., NLTK), must use a machine learning library (e.g., scikit-learn), and must be under 300 lines of code. The evaluation criteria are: (1) accuracy of sentiment analysis (40 points), (2) accuracy of aspect identification (30 points), (3) efficiency of the algorithm (15 points), (4) maintainability of code (5 points), and (5) adherence to best practices (10 points).
constraints: Must use a NLP library (e.g., NLTK)
Must use a machine learning library (e.g., scikit-learn)
Must be under 300 lines of code

evaluationCriteria.description: Evaluates the accuracy of sentiment analysis using aspect-based sentiment analysis.
Assesses the accuracy of aspect identification in product reviews.
Checks the efficiency of the algorithm in terms of computation time and memory usage.
Evaluates the maintainability of the code in terms of readability, modularity, and testability.
Checks if the code adheres to best practices in terms of security, performance, and code organization.
